commands.review_summary:
The branch filters malformed realtime custom tool names in the shared Talk resolver and in OpenAI Realtime and Google Live provider payload builders, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +167. Total +216 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main reads custom realtime tool names directly and forwards provider payloads without vendor contract filtering. I did not open a live provider session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers need to resolve the draft/waiting state and decide whether live provider smoke proof is required before merge.

target.title: fix(talk): quarantine malformed realtime tool names
target.body:
## Summary
- Skip unreadable, blank, or whitespace custom realtime tool names in the shared agent-consult tool resolver without dropping provider-specific valid names.
- Quarantine OpenAI realtime tools against the OpenAI name contract before WebSocket session updates and browser/WebRTC client-secret sessions.
- Quarantine Google Live function declarations against the Google function-name contract before connect config creation.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/talk/agent-consult-tool.ts src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/talk/agent-consult-tool.ts src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- Attempted AWS Crabbox changed gate, but it did not start remotely because the local sync-prep worktree failed with `No space left on device` before upload.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: malformed custom realtime voice tool names no longer crash or poison realtime provider session payload construction, while valid provider-specific OpenAI and Google names stay available.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest shards for the shared talk helper, OpenAI realtime provider, and Google realtime provider on this branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/google/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts --reporter=dot`; targeted oxfmt; targeted oxlint; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; branch autoreview against `origin/main`.

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 3 shards / 83 tests; targeted formatter, lint, and diff checks passed; branch autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings on final head `c1ce64caa3bd50aee360fd77a4f7eb7e3fe9da65`.

Observed result after fix: unreadable and malformed realtime tool names are skipped before OpenAI WebSocket, OpenAI browser/WebRTC, and Google Live provider payloads are built; OpenAI-valid `1_lookup` and Google-valid `calendar.lookup:next` remain exposed on their respective provider paths.

What was not tested: AWS Crabbox `check:changed` did not run because local disk exhaustion prevented Crabbox from preparing its sync checkout; no live OpenAI or Google realtime provider session was opened.
target.author: vincentkoc
